steps.title: Identify what Sara is doing when telling stories
steps.content: When Sara “began to invent wonderful things,” the passage immediately shows her action: she “made what she told lovely or alarming by the raising or dropping of her voice.” She's composing stories through enthusiastic, dramatic performance.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the answer
steps.content:
Create is the exact word for this action—composing stories spontaneously.

The other choices don't work: discovering means finding something that already exists; misleading suggests deception contradicting her enthusiasm; disguising implies concealment unsupported by the text.

That confirms C is correct.
